hero.subtitle: Crafting games and tools in Unity — from VR experiences and AR applications to indie multiplayer games with clean, intentional architecture.

about.headline: Building games that
paragraphs: I've spent years in Unity across a range of disciplines — VR locomotion systems, AR enterprise tools, multiplayer architecture, and performant UI. My focus is always on code that's clean, maintainable, and a joy to build on top of.

Outside of client work, I'm building indie games that matter to me: deterministic networked shooters, spellcasting brawlers, and a quiet story about plush toys and family. The hobby projects inform the professional ones.

bullets: Developing performant, scalable UI systems using Unity's UI Toolkit and custom tooling
Architecting editor tools to accelerate the content pipeline for children's interactive books
Implementing dependency injection patterns with VContainer for clean, testable code
Async programming with UniTask for non-blocking asset loading and animations
Built augmented reality experiences for enterprise clients
Created custom Unity editor tools to streamline artist and designer workflows
Collaborated cross-functionally with design and product teams on AR pipelines
Covered League of Legends competitive scene as specialist journalist
Produced match analyses, player profiles, and tournament deep-dives
Developed VR experiences and interactive applications in Unity
Implemented networked multiplayer features using Photon Fusion
Worked on gameplay systems, VR locomotion, and user interactions

items.title: Orbital Duel
items.description: A spaceship PvP shooter where movement is constrained to orbits — ships rotate around a gravitational centre and shift lanes inward or outward to attack.

techs.label: Unity
techs.desc: Game engine used for the entire project — rendering, physics, input, and all gameplay systems.

techs.label: C#
techs.desc: Primary language for all gameplay logic, movement systems, and game state management.

techs.label: Photon Quantum
techs.desc: Deterministic networking framework ensuring pixel-perfect, lag-free multiplayer across all clients.

techs.label: Multiplayer Architecture
techs.desc: Designed around a server-authoritative loop — all orbital positions and combat resolved deterministically.

techs.label: Retro Pixel Aesthetic
techs.desc: Custom pixel-style visuals to give the game a classic arcade feel while running on modern netcode.

items.title: Spell Brawl
items.description: A platformer brawler with a unique two-phase loop — draft spells into your spellbook during Pick phase, then unleash them in order during Fight phase.

techs.label: Unity
techs.desc: Full game engine foundation — handles 2D physics, scene management, and all rendering.

techs.label: C#
techs.desc: All gameplay systems — phase management, spellbook logic, ability sequencing, and player controllers.

techs.label: VContainer
techs.desc: Dependency injection framework — decouples systems so the Fight and Pick phases are independently testable.

techs.label: UniTask
techs.desc: Async/await framework for Unity — drives phase transitions, ability animations, and UI sequences without coroutines.

techs.label: DOTween
techs.desc: Animation library for spell effects, UI transitions, and the satisfying feel of the spellbook drafting UI.

techs.label: Multiplayer
techs.desc: Networked game loop synchronising the Pick and Fight phases across all connected players in real time.

items.title: Plush Keeper
items.description: A warm emotional simulator — run a plush shop inherited from your estranged father, build a relationship through daily shop life, and discover why the hobby matters.

techs.label: Unity
techs.desc: Engine powering the cozy shop sim — 2D scene management, save system, and all visual presentation.

techs.label: C#
techs.desc: Shop logic, inventory systems, narrative state machine, and character interaction systems.

techs.label: DOTween
techs.desc: Animates the shop's cozy feel — plush bounces, dialogue popups, and gentle UI transitions.

techs.label: Narrative Design
techs.desc: Story unfolds through shop interactions — relationship with the father deepens as the player progresses days.

techs.label: Blender
techs.desc: Used for 3D asset modelling of the plush characters and shop props that define the game's visual identity.

skills.heading: Everything I
skills.subtitle: The engines, libraries, patterns and tools I reach for.

contact.heading: Let's make
contact.subtitle: Open to new roles in game development. Based in Hungary, open to remote.
